questions.text: The war council has gathered to find a way to break a siege that has held your walls for a month.

options.label: You table half a dozen schemes before the wine is even poured
You offer a couple of solid ideas worth weighing
You have one thought, and you share it
You wait to hear what the others propose
You have nothing to add; you would rather follow a plan

questions.text: You stand in the ruins of a keep burned in the last war, asked what might be made of it.

options.label: You picture the whole court restored, down to the banners on the wall
You imagine it clearly enough to describe it
You see roughly what it might become
You struggle to picture much beyond the rubble
You see only broken stone; imagining more is wasted effort

questions.text: A maester lays out a tangled inheritance dispute with three claimants and a contested will.

options.label: You grasp the whole tangle before he finishes speaking
You follow it readily
You get there after a moment's thought
You need it explained a second time
You let the maester handle it; such things lose you

questions.text: An evening at court turns into a long debate on the nature of justice and the duties of a king.

options.label: Talk of principles bores you; you want to know who pays and who bleeds
You have little patience for it, but you sit through it
You will engage if it turns to something practical
You find the ideas worth chewing on
You relish the debate for its own sake

questions.text: Your captain asks you to dream up a ruse to draw an enemy host out of its position.

options.label: Nothing comes to you; invention simply is not in you
You manage little; cleverness like that escapes you
You can force out a plain, obvious trick
A decent ruse takes shape after some thought
Three schemes spring to mind at once

questions.text: An enemy army is days from your gates and the defense is yours to ready.

options.label: Every contingency drilled, the stores counted twice
A solid plan is in place and the men know it
A rough plan, good enough to start with
You will improvise once they actually arrive
You had not really given it much thought

questions.text: A treaty is set before you to sign, its terms drafted by the other side's clerks.

options.label: You catch every clause and the caveats buried in the margins
You read it closely and flag the key terms
You skim it for the main points
You trust that someone else has read it
You sign where they tell you to sign

questions.text: The running of a great keep and its household has fallen to you.

options.label: Everything in its place, every duty rostered and checked
You keep things mostly tidy and the duties assigned
Order where it matters, slack everywhere else
You let the place run loose and sort trouble as it comes
Chaos suits you fine; order is just a cage

questions.text: You are handed charge of the winter grain stores, ledgers and all.

options.label: Records lost and sacks miscounted; it is a shambles by month's end
Things slip and tangle more often than not
A few errors creep in, nothing ruinous
It mostly runs clean
You leave it flawless, every ledger matched to the grain

questions.text: A long, tedious night watch on the walls falls to you.

options.label: You find someone else to stand it; your duty can wait
You dodge it whenever you can
You do it, grudgingly and late
You see it through without complaint
Your duty is yours and yours alone; you would never pass it off

questions.text: A feast fills the great hall, the wine flowing and the tables loud.

options.label: You are at the center of it, leading the toasts and the tales
You are lively and draw people in around you
You enjoy yourself among a few good companions
You keep to the edges of the hall
You leave early; crowds like this drain you

questions.text: You arrive among strangers at a rival lord's court, knowing no one in the room.

options.label: You are introducing yourself before your cloak is even off
You readily strike up talk with those nearby
You will speak if there is good reason to
You wait to be approached
You say nothing at all unless forced to

questions.text: The hall falls silent and every face turns to hear the judgment you are about to give.

options.label: You welcome every eye; the floor is yours
You are comfortable holding the room
You manage it well enough
You would rather the attention move on quickly
You shrink from it and wish another would speak

questions.text: A gathering of lords jockeys for the king's favor across a crowded room.

options.label: You stay in the shadows, watching, saying little
You hang back more often than not
You step forward only when it serves you
You make yourself known to the room
You put yourself front and center of it all

questions.text: You are seated through dinner beside a foreign envoy you have never met.

options.label: You hold your tongue and let the silence sit
You speak only when spoken to
You offer a few careful words
You chat with them easily enough
You are talking like old friends within minutes

questions.text: A soldier breaks down before you, undone by the death of a close friend in the fighting.

options.label: You feel his grief as your own and sit with him
You are moved, and you offer him comfort
You acknowledge his loss and move on
You note it but feel little yourself
Grief is weakness; you tell him to stand

questions.text: A smallfolk petitioner begs your help while you are already buried in your own work.

options.label: You set your work aside and hear them out fully
You make the time to help
You give them a brief moment
You send them on to someone else
You wave them off; you have your own concerns

questions.text: A frightened hostage, newly taken, sits stiff and silent at your table.

options.label: You go out of your way to put them at ease
You are warm and welcoming toward them
You are civil enough
You ignore their unease entirely
You let them stew in their fear

questions.text: A rival lord needles you with a sly remark in open court, hoping for a rise.

options.label: You cut him down with the cruelest words you have
You return the insult sharply
You give a measured barb in reply
You let it pass without answer
You answer his spite with plain courtesy

questions.text: You find yourself at a gathering full of new faces and unfamiliar names.

options.label: Other people's lives do not interest you in the least
You have little curiosity about any of them
You will listen if it happens to concern you
You are fairly curious about the people here
You want to know everyone's story before the night is out

questions.text: Two messengers arrive at once, each bearing word of a crisis breaking in a different quarter.

options.label: Your nerves fray on the instant; the pressure undoes you
You feel the strain of it hard
You are rattled, but still functioning
You stay fairly steady through it
Pressure like this barely touches you

questions.text: It is the night before a dangerous parley whose outcome you cannot be sure of.

options.label: You lie awake turning over every way it could go wrong
You worry over it well into the night
It crosses your mind, then you sleep
You give it little thought
You sleep soundly; worry changes nothing

questions.text: A single trying day at court brings one setback and small triumph after another.

options.label: Your temper lurches from high to low and back again
Your mood shifts often through the day
You have your ups and downs
You stay mostly even
Your humor holds steady from dawn to dusk

questions.text: You go about the ordinary strains of rule, the daily weight of decisions and demands.

options.label: You are at ease most days, whatever comes
You are generally relaxed
Calm on some days, tense on others
You are often on edge
You are rarely relaxed at all

questions.text: A long, grim winter settles over the keep, the halls cold and the days short.

options.label: Low spirits seldom find you; you keep your humor
You rarely sink into gloom
You have your darker stretches now and then
Melancholy visits you often
The gloom rarely lifts from you at all
